issues.description: Finish consolidating the canonical Go task, intake, and workflow vocabulary so downstream runtime slices stop inventing parallel compatibility contracts.
issues.title: Unified domain model and intake contract migration

issues.description: Keep porting governance, execution-contract, audit, and policy semantics into Go so review and approval behavior no longer depends on Python-only code paths.
issues.title: Risk, policy, and approval semantics migration

issues.description: Close the Go execution loop with workflow-aware orchestration, journal state, and runtime closeout so active development no longer needs the Python workflow engine.
issues.title: Workflow orchestration and scheduler loop migration

issues.description: Move runtime evidence, reporting, and weekly operations closeout onto Go-owned observability outputs that line up with the new execution loop.
issues.title: Observability, reporting, and weekly operations surface migration

issues.description: Finish moving operator-facing status, control-center, and triage endpoints to Go once the workflow runtime and reporting surfaces are stable.
issues.title: Control center, triage, and operations view migration

issues.description: Port the remaining repo board, registry, gateway, linkage, and lineage surfaces into Go without leaking repo-specific concerns into the scheduler runtime.
issues.title: Repo collaboration and lineage surface migration

comments.body:
What changed:
Added a Go-native local tracker closeout path in bigclawctl so local issues can be transitioned and annotated without manual JSON edits. The command now appends completion-style comments with change summary, validation, commit SHA, and PR metadata, and the local issue store preserves those fields on write. Blocker: this workspace origin is a local path (/Users/jxrt/Desktop/symphony-main/BigClaw), so no GitHub PR URL can be refreshed or recorded from the current repo config.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Added a general Go-native local tracker update path in bigclawctl so local issues can be moved between states and annotated with progress comments without manual JSON edits. The local issue store now supports state-only updates, comment-only updates, or combined mutations while preserving existing tracker fields.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Fixed a Go GitHub sync edge case where a fresh issue branch on origin could remain unpublished if local HEAD matched the remote default-branch SHA. EnsureRepoSync now still performs the first push when the issue branch is missing, and a regression test covers that branch-publication path.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/githubsync
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Ported the clean fast-forward parity case from the legacy Python GitHub sync tests into the Go suite. The Go sync toolchain now explicitly proves it can fast-forward a stale clean branch to origin before deciding whether any push is necessary.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/githubsync
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Ported the missing workspace-bootstrap parity cases from the legacy Python suite into the Go tests. The Go bootstrap layer now has coverage for warm-cache reuse, existing-worktree reuse, cleanup cache preservation, stale-seed recovery, and validation-report aggregation, plus stronger assertions on shared git-dir and remote wiring.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/bootstrap
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Added CLI-level coverage for the Go workspace validation entrypoint. bigclawctl tests now verify that `workspace validate` emits the expected JSON report payload and can write the markdown validation report used by unattended operator flows.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Added command-layer coverage for Go github-sync flag behavior. bigclawctl tests now verify that `github-sync status --require-clean` returns a nonzero CLI exit while still emitting status payloads for dirty repos, and that `github-sync sync --allow-dirty` reports repo state without failing.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Added command-level regression coverage for Go github-sync `--require-synced` behavior. The CLI now has tests proving `status --require-synced` fails with JSON SHA evidence for an unpushed commit, and `sync --require-synced` succeeds only after publishing a missing issue branch and verifying remote branch parity.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/githubsync
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Hardened Go workspace bootstrap reuse validation so existing workspaces are checked for branch, shared seed git-common-dir, and origin drift before the seed repair path can normalize those mismatches. Added regression coverage for standalone clone rejection plus wrong-branch and wrong-origin reuse cases.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/bootstrap
passed
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/bootstrap
passed

comments.body:
What changed:
Hardened Go refill operator behavior so `--refresh-url` now fails fast on HTTP error responses instead of silently ignoring dashboard refresh failures after a promotion. Added command-level regression coverage proving successful refresh POSTs are triggered and 5xx refresh responses abort the refill run with status detail.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl ./internal/refill
passed
comments.pr_url: unavailable (origin is /Users/jxrt/Desktop/symphony-main/BigClaw; gh cannot resolve a GitHub host)

comments.body:
What changed:
Added Python-compatible `workspace validate` flag aliases to Go bigclawctl so unattended callers can keep using `--report-file` and `--no-cleanup` during the bootstrap-validator cutover. Added CLI coverage proving the report-file alias writes markdown output and the no-cleanup alias preserves issue workspaces after validation.

Validation:
cd bigclaw-go && go test ./cmd/bigclawctl
passed

issues.description: Keep hardening the Go-first bootstrap, refill, and GitHub sync toolchain so unattended multi-issue work no longer relies on Python entrypoints or Linear-only operators.
issues.title: Workflow, bootstrap, and GitHub sync toolchain migration

issues.description: Freeze or retire the remaining Python runtime entrypoints only after the Go workflow, observability, and repo collaboration surfaces have matching owners and validation.
issues.title: Python deprecation and Go-only mainline switch
